limitations: ai_investment_intensity: CMS Energy is a utility company; AI is discussed primarily as an external demand driver (data centers) and an integration challenge, not as a direct R&D or capital investment target. No AI-attributable dollar amounts or named AI investment programs are disclosed in the excerpts. Score reflects generic statements about integrating AI technology.

ai_narrative_concreteness: The 1 GW data center agreement is a concrete named initiative but is framed as a customer load event, not an AI deployment by CMS itself. AI use cases internal to CMS operations are not described beyond drone technology (not explicitly AI-labeled) and general integration language.

ai_risk_disclosure_depth: Risk-section and MD&A material is present. Risks are company-specific with named mechanisms (capital market tightening, demand shortfall, contract non-fulfillment) but no quantification or named governance/oversight bodies for AI risk specifically.
capex_mentions: No AI-attributable dollar amounts found in the excerpts; the $100M statewide aid and 1 GW data center references are not CMS capex figures tied to AI investment.
